skill.name: session-template
skill.description: Applies task-specific planning templates for AI coding sessions, including feature, bugfix, refactor, documentation, and security workflows.
skill.summary: Create structured AI session plans from task-specific coding templates.
skill.author: Atyantik

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review found Markdown workflow examples and planning template text rather than executable malware. The only real risk factor is documented local command usage for ai-update-plan, which is legitimate but should remain visible to users.

low_findings.title: Local Command Examples Require User Awareness
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with low severity. The skill documents local ai-update-plan and ai-start-task commands. They are intended workflow commands and do not show network access, secret access, or destructive behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible Markdown examples and use local project tooling. Risk remains low because an agent may execute them in the user workspace.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain template headings, design language, documentation words, or checklist text. No cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review confirms these locations are Markdown planning content. They do not call crypto libraries or implement hashing.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The flagged lines are planning checklist items such as make check, safety checkpoint, and avoid over-engineering. No system or network reconnaissance behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain workflow text with no scanning commands, network calls, or host enumeration instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell examples for local plan management. These commands are not malicious, but users should know the skill may ask the agent to run local CLI commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command snippets are explicit and task-related. They are not obfuscated, remote, or destructive.

content.user_title: Structure AI Coding Sessions With Templates
content.value_statement: AI coding sessions often start without a clear plan, which causes missed testing and review steps. This skill applies structured templates for common engineering work.
actual_capabilities: Selects a feature, bugfix, refactor, documentation, or security template from task context.
Turns generic checklist phases into task-specific plan items.
Uses ai-update-plan examples to add, rename, remove, and show plan items.
Encourages TDD phases, quality checks, documentation updates, and final review.
Provides guidance for custom templates, placeholders, and multi-template workflows.
limitations: It provides planning guidance, not direct code implementation.
It assumes ai-update-plan or compatible local tooling is available.
It does not validate templates with a parser or schema.
It may need manual pruning for small or unusual tasks.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Feature
use_cases.description: Create a phased plan for research, tests, implementation, quality checks, documentation, and review.

use_cases.title: Control a Bugfix Session
use_cases.description: Structure reproduction, root cause analysis, regression tests, the fix, and final verification.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Workflows
use_cases.description: Use consistent planning templates across coding, documentation, refactoring, and security tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Apply a Basic Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the best session template for this task: fix the login redirect bug.

prompt_templates.title: Customize a Feature Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the feature template for adding OAuth2 login, then customize each phase for authentication risks.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an Existing Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current plan and apply the refactor template only where it adds useful missing steps.

prompt_templates.title: Combine Complex Templates
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a combined security and refactor session plan for hardening the authorization module.

output_examples.input: Add passwordless login to the application.
output: A feature development plan with research, TDD, implementation, quality, documentation, and final review phases.
Task-specific items for authentication flow, token handling, provider integration, and manual verification.

output_examples.input: Fix a memory leak in the data processor.
output: A bugfix plan focused on reproduction, root cause analysis, failing tests, minimal fix, and regression prevention.
Extra checks for performance impact and full test-suite verification.
A documentation plan covering content audit, structure, examples, review, code sync, and validation.
Checklist items for verifying examples and updating related AI documentation.

best_practices: Apply the template before implementation begins, then tailor every generic item to the task.
Remove checklist items that do not apply, so the plan stays useful and clear.
Keep testing, quality checks, and documentation steps visible until the task is complete.
anti_patterns: Do not keep every template item when the task is small or unrelated.
Do not treat templates as a substitute for reading project documentation.
Do not run local command examples without understanding the current workspace context.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It applies structured planning templates to AI coding sessions for common engineering tasks.

faq.question: Does it write code for me?
faq.answer: No. It creates and customizes a plan that guides later coding work.

faq.question: Which templates are included?
faq.answer: It includes feature, bugfix, refactor, documentation, and security templates.

faq.question: Does it require local tools?
faq.answer: The guidance references ai-update-plan and ai-start-task commands for plan management.

faq.question: Can I customize the templates?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes custom templates, placeholders, conditional sections, and combined workflows.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found local command examples but no malicious intent, network exfiltration, or prompt injection.
